summary: Smoke + integration tests for Diamond Rivals: all REST endpoints pass, full WebSocket 2-player game simulated end-to-end with correct rewards persistence, and lobby UI flow (nickname -> team select -> queue) works against the public preview URL.
passed: POST /api/guest creates profile with coins=250 and items=[classic_cap]
POST /api/profile/{id}/daily awards +100 coins; second claim same day -> 400
GET /api/leaderboard returns sorted list with rank
WebSocket: connected -> queued -> match_start -> at_bat -> pitch_result -> match_over flow verified with 2 concurrent clients
Full 3-inning game completed; winner +150 coins / loser +50 coins persisted; wins/losses incremented correctly in MongoDB
Frontend lobby loads with hero 'DIAMOND RIVALS'; nickname-input, start-game-btn, team-card-NYC, quick-match-btn all visible and functional
After clicking quick-match-btn, SEARCHING... state appears (WS connection established and queued)
notes: Two-tab live match UI (call-pitch/call-swing, scoreboard updates, match-over-modal) was NOT exercised via Playwright due to two-context overhead; however, the underlying WebSocket flow that powers it was fully validated via direct WS clients (all message types: match_start, at_bat, pitch_result, match_over received and resolved correctly). Test credentials: N/A (guest sessions).
